name: Bill Frisell
abstract: William Richard Frisell ( fri-ZEL; born March 18, 1951) is an American jazz guitarist. He first came to prominence at ECM Records in the 1980s, as both a session player and a leader.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: "The Elephant Sleeps But Still Remembers” is one of my favorites of the projects that I did with Bill Frisell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: We, we did a double CD and half of it is just Bill and me... Bill and I picked out this beautiful drawing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...like Pat Metheny and Bill Frisell. But if you could talk about Hendrix, that would be, I think, valuable. [INTERVIEWEE] He had a great advantage...
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah. Dave Samuels, Formanek, Frisell – a very young Bill Frisell, probably. Yes, yes. I forgot that Bill was on that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
